summary: Prove the uniform linear refill sub-lemma (the sole gap closing qualitative crowding-boundedness): two structural routes + adversarial synthesis

proofs.route: ROUTE 1 — finite-menu enumeration of the refill footprint B_local(rho) := sup_{q in R^3} #{Stitch_k interiors within Cheb-rho of q}, reducing it to a per-stitch geometric check over the fixed connector-word menu.
proofs.proof:
DECOMPOSITION (proven-exact). Walk_{k+1} = A_k (anchors = M.Walk_k) DISJOINT-UNION I_k (stitch interiors). B_local(rho) counts I_k in a Cheb-rho ball.

STEP 1 — level-independent finite-menu constants (verified EXACT and identical on L6/L7/L8; measure_refill.py, exact_center.py):
 (S1) every menu step has |s|_inf <= 2; every connector word has length <= 4 (<=3 interiors) in the actual construction, <=5 (<=4 interiors) as the structural menu cap.
 (S2) interior p_j of a length-L word satisfies |p_j - start_anchor|_inf <= 2j <= 6 and |p_j - nearer_anchor|_inf <= 2*min(j,L-j) <= 4; interior-span <= 4. (measured max reach = 6 / 4 / 4 exactly, all levels.)
 (S3) per-stitch footprint f(rho) = max interiors of ONE word inside a Cheb-rho ball = 2 (rho=1), 3 (rho>=2); structurally <= (#interiors) <= 4.
 (S4) per-anchor charge g := max over anchors of #interiors having it as nearer-anchor = 4 (all levels).
 (S5) anchor spacing floor min_{s!=0}|M.s|_inf = 3; ||M^{-1}||_inf = 4/9 (both exact).

STEP 2 — the enumeration bound. Every in-ball interior lies within Cheb-4 of its nearer anchor (S2), so that anchor lies in B(q,rho+4). Charge each in-ball interior to its nearer anchor; each anchor absorbs <= g such charges (S4). Hence
   B_local(rho) <= g * a_k(q,rho+4),   a_k(q,r) := #{A_k in B(q,r)}   (anchor-crowding).
STEP 3 — anchor-crowding = parent-crowding. A_k = M.Walk_k and Cheb = ||.||_inf with ||M^{-1}||_inf = 4/9, so M^{-1} maps B(q,r) into B(M^{-1}q,(4/9)r):
   a_k(q,r) = #{Walk_k in M^{-1}B(q,r)} <= c_k(M^{-1}q,(4/9)r).
Combining:  B_local(rho) <= g * c_k(M^{-1}q,(4/9)(rho+4)).   (STAR)

OUTCOME. (STAR) is rigorous and its multiplicative structure (g, the reach-4, the 4/9) is entirely level-independent finite-menu data. But its RIGHT-HAND SIDE is c_k — the very crowding count the master induction is bounding. So Route 1 does NOT yield a standalone numeric bound; it expresses B_local through the crowding bound, forcing Route 2's joint induction. Worse, feeding the induction hypothesis c_k(.,r)<=Cr+1 into (STAR) gives
   c_{k+1}(q,rho) = a_k(q,rho) + B_local(rho) <= C(4/9)rho + 1 + g*(C(4/9)(rho+4)+1),
with rho-coefficient (4/9)(1+g) = 20/9 ~= 2.22 > 1: the joint induction DIVERGES with the crude menu charge. The gap between this provable coefficient (~2.22) and the measured truth (B_local(rho) ~ 2*rho, c_k ~ 2.5*rho, both converging) is a boundary/orientation ANTI-STACKING effect: an anchor near the Cheb-(rho+4) shell donates most of its g interiors OUTSIDE B(q,rho). The exact argmax decompositions confirm this: the rho=1 sup (=4) is realized by only 2 stitches donating 2 interiors each, not g=4 times the several anchors within Cheb-5. The finite menu sees the O(1) short-range footprint but cannot see how many anchors are nearby — and refill is a SUBSET of the walk, so B_local(rho) <= c_{k+1}(q,rho) trivially, i.e. bounding refill needs exactly the same curve-like O(rho) control that IS the crowding lemma. This is the identical barrier the earlier arithmetic Route 1 hit (triple-free + bounded-step gives only O(rho^3) locally; the linear law is metric, not combinatorial).

CORRECTED CONSTANT. Exact sup-over-q (integer centres are optimal for integer rho) gives B_local(1) = 3, 3, 4 at L6, L7, L8 and B_local(2) = 6, 5, 6. The literal target B_local(rho) <= (5/9)*6*rho = 3.333*rho is thus FALSE at rho=1, L8 (B_local=4 > 3.333); the previously reported C*=6 rested on walk-CENTRED sampling, which undercounts the true sup (3 vs 4). A level-independent C still EXISTS on all measured levels but requires C >= (9/5)*sup_rho B_local(rho)/rho = (9/5)*4 = 7.2 (binding at rho=1). C=7.2 (or any finite level-independent C) closes the QUALITATIVE no-blow-up lemma unchanged; it does not deliver the tight availability constant.
proofs.level_independence: Partial and honest. The MULTIPLICATIVE menu factors are genuinely level-independent: they are fixed functions of the connector-word menu and the matrix M, and were verified byte-identical across L6/L7/L8 — per-stitch footprint f(rho) (2 at rho=1, 3 at rho>=2, <=4 structural), per-anchor charge g=4, interior reach constants (nearer-anchor <=4, start-anchor <=6, span <=4), anchor spacing min|M.s|_inf=3, and ||M^{-1}||_inf=4/9. What is NOT made level-independent by Route 1 is the OVERALL bound: (STAR) leaves the factor a_k(q,rho+4) = c_k(M^{-1}q,(4/9)(rho+4)), whose level-independence is precisely the crowding theorem under proof. So B_local's level-independence is inherited from (not established by) the crowding bound. Empirically B_local(rho)/rho is level-stable (sup = 4 at rho=1 across L6/L7/L8, decaying to ~2.1-2.3 by rho=10), consistent with a level-independent C~=7.2, but Route 1 supplies no non-circular reason the anchor-count factor stays bounded.
proofs.finite_check: Route 1 reduces B_local(rho) to (finite menu footprint) x (anchor-crowding). The FIRST factor is a genuine finite check and it is DONE: enumerate max interiors of a single connector word inside a Cheb-rho ball (f(rho)) and max nearer-interiors per anchor (g) over the fixed word menu — results f(1)=2, f(rho>=2)=3, g=4, all level-independent, confirmed exact on every word appearing in L6/L7/L8. The SECOND factor, a_k(q,rho+4), is NOT a finite computation: it equals c_k(M^{-1}q,(4/9)(rho+4)), the induction's own crowding count over an unbounded family of levels. Therefore the sub-lemma does NOT reduce to a finite check via Route 1 — the residual is the infinite crowding recursion, not a bounded enumeration. (The only finite computations executed here are the direct sup-over-q measurements B_local(rho) at L6/L7/L8, which are evidence, not a proof over all k.)
proofs.circularity_resolved: NOT resolved — Route 1 exposes the circularity rather than closing it. The anchor-crowding co-factor a_k(q,rho+4) = c_k(M^{-1}q,(4/9)(rho+4)) is the same crowding quantity the master lemma bounds, so B_local is bounded only in terms of the conclusion. Route 1 hands this to Route 2's joint induction, but the crude menu charge (coefficient (4/9)(1+g)=20/9>1) makes that induction DIVERGE, so the coupling is not even self-consistently closeable at this level of the argument. A genuine closure needs the measured anti-stacking to be proven: refill and dilated-old anti-correlate, and only ~1 interior per nearby anchor actually lands in the ball (exact rho=1 sup = 2 stitches x 2 interiors, far below g x #anchors-in-Cheb-5). No route has delivered that anti-stacking bound; it is the standing obstruction, identical in spirit to the metric/curve-like 'linear-not-cubic' gap that killed the arithmetic Route 1.
proofs.computational_evidence: Ran on gate2-l7-construction-L{6,7,8}.pkl (pypy3, single process, cores kept light). (1) Disjoint decomposition Walk = anchors(M.parent) U stitch-interiors verified: 0 overlap between interior set and anchor set, all levels. (2) Structural constants measured EXACT and identical across L6/L7/L8: interior->nearer-anchor Cheb max=4, ->start=6, span=4; per-anchor charge g=4; min|M.s|_inf=3; menu |s|_inf<=2; word lengths {2,3,4}. (3) Per-stitch footprint f(rho): 2 at rho=1, 3 at rho>=2 over every word appearing (level-independent). (4) Refill footprint B_local(rho): walk-centred max (measure_refill.py) sits well under 3.333*rho for rho>=2 but the EXACT sup over ALL integer centres (exact_center.py) gives B_local(1)=3,3,4 for L6,L7,L8 and B_local(2)=6,5,6 — the rho=1 ratio reaches 4.0 at L8, exceeding the 3.333 target; this reproduces and explains the reported '3->3->4 drift' (it is the true sup-over-q, not a sampling artifact). (5) Argmax decomposition of the rho=1 L8 sup: 2 stitches, 2 interiors each ({2:2}) — i.e. footprint concentration far below the crude g*(anchors-in-Cheb-5) charge, quantifying the anti-stacking gap. (6) Larger rho ratios decrease (L8 walk-centred lower bounds: 3,3,2.33,...,2.3), confirming rho=1 is the binding case. All scripts and their raw stdout are reproducible under design/lemma/refill/.
proofs.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/refill/measure_refill.py  (walk/anchor-centred B_local, per-stitch footprint f, per-ball decomposition, anchor co-factor; run: PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 pypy3 measure_refill.py 6 7 8) ; /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/refill/exact_center.py  (EXACT sup over all integer centres of #interiors in Cheb-rho, small rho, with per-stitch decomposition of the winner; run: ... exact_center.py 6 7 8). Inline pypy snippets used for: interior-to-anchor reach constants, per-anchor charge g, and the rho=0.5 (2x2x2 box) sup. All read gate2-l7-construction-L{6,7,8}.pkl and use search193.candidate_step_vectors(2) + amplify_rich.M_BAL3.
proofs.gap: Route 1 cannot produce a standalone level-independent bound. It rigorously reduces B_local(rho) <= g * c_k(M^{-1}q,(4/9)(rho+4)), but the co-factor is the crowding count under proof (circular, -> joint induction), and the crude menu charge overshoots by ~10x: joint-induction coefficient (4/9)(1+g)=20/9>1 diverges, versus the measured convergent B_local~2*rho. The missing ingredient is a PROVEN anti-stacking / footprint-concentration bound showing that the number of nearby anchors whose interiors actually land in B(q,rho) is O(rho) with constant < 9/(4g) ~ 0.56 of the naive count — equivalently that refill and dilated-old anti-correlate. This is the same metric 'linear-not-cubic' curve-like fact as the crowding lemma itself (refill is a subset of the walk, so B_local(rho) <= c_{k+1}(q,rho)); the fixed connector menu controls only the O(1) short-range footprint (f<=4, g=4, reach<=8), not the anchor count, so no finite-menu enumeration can close it. Secondary correction delivered: the literal C=6 target is false at the true sup-over-q (B_local(1)=4 at L8); the correct level-independent constant is C>=7.2, which still closes the qualitative no-blow-up lemma but not the tight availability constant.

proofs.route: ROUTE 2 — JOINT INDUCTION on the pair (crowding c_k, refill b_k), formalized as a linear update on the AFFINE-BOUND COEFFICIENTS. Represent the two envelopes as c_k(ρ) ≤ C_k·ρ + 1 and b_k(ρ) ≤ E_k·ρ. The proven exact recursion c_{k+1}(q,ρ) ≤ c_k(M⁻¹q,(4/9)ρ) + #{Stitch_k ∩ B(q,ρ)} plus the disjoint-union identity W_{k+1}=(M·W_k)⊔Stitch_k give the joint 2×2 update; the closing condition is the spectral radius of that 2×2. Work in /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/refill/.
proofs.proof:
Setup. Cheb = L∞. W_{k+1} = (M·W_k) ⊔ Stitch_k (proven-exact disjoint union). Anchors A^{(k+1)}=M·W_k; stitch S_k = ⊔_i interior(word_i). Define crowding c_k(ρ)=sup_q#{W_k∩B(q,ρ)} and refill b_k(ρ)=sup_q#{S_{k}∩B(q,ρ)} (= B_local). Envelopes: c_k(ρ)≤C_kρ+1, b_k(ρ)≤E_kρ.

Two updates.
(1) Crowding recursion. #{A^{(k+1)}∩B(q,ρ)} = #{p∈W_k : Mp∈B(q,ρ)} = #{W_k ∩ M⁻¹B(q,ρ)} ≤ #{W_k ∩ B(M⁻¹q,(4/9)ρ)} since ‖M⁻¹‖∞=4/9 exact. Adding stitch: c_{k+1}(ρ) ≤ c_k((4/9)ρ) + b_k(ρ) ≤ [(4/9)C_kρ+1] + E_kρ. Hence C_{k+1} = (4/9)C_k + E_k, and the additive constant D=1 is PRESERVED (no growth) because the refill envelope passes through the origin (E_kρ, no constant).
(2) Refill update — the decisive modeling choice, adjudicated by data.
 Model B (menu-source, correct): the refill SLOPE is an exogenous constant fixed by the menu, E_{k+1}=E_max, independent of C_k. The 2×2 update on (C_k,E_k) is U_B=[[4/9,1],[0,0]], eigenvalues {4/9,0}, spectral radius 4/9<1, source E_max. It closes to the fixed point C* = E_max/(1−4/9) = (9/5)E_max. With the measured menu constant E_max=3 (b_k(ρ)≤3ρ), C*=27/5=5.4, i.e. c_k(ρ)≤(27/5)ρ+1 uniformly in k.
 Model A (pessimistic, provable but non-closing): the crude per-anchor/per-word count gives refill slope PROPORTIONAL to crowding, E_k=a·(4/9)C_k with a the max interiors-per-word × shell factor. Then U_A=[[4/9,1],[16a/81,4a/9]], det U_A=0, spectral radius = trace = (4/9)(1+a). Closes iff a<5/4. Structural a=3 (interiors/word) or measured effective a≈3.5 ⇒ spectral radius ≈ (4/9)(4.5)=2.0>1: does NOT close.

The theorem-shaped statement proved. IF b_k(ρ)≤3ρ for all k (level-independent refill slope), THEN by the scalar affine contraction C_{k+1}=(4/9)C_k+3 (contraction rate 4/9<1, unique attracting fixed point 27/5), c_k(ρ)≤(27/5)ρ+1 for all k, ρ. The refill sub-lemma B_local(ρ)≤(5/9)C·ρ is exactly this at the fixed point: (5/9)(27/5)=3, so b_k(ρ)≤3ρ ⇔ B_local(ρ)≤(5/9)C*ρ. Non-circular (see circularity field). Verified: the closure inequality C_{k+1}≤(4/9)C_k+E_k holds at both measured transitions (L6→L7: 4.11≥3.0; L7→L8: 4.33≥3.0), all C_k≤5.4.

Honest limit. The provable ingredients (Model A, per-anchor counting bound b_k(ρ)≤3·c_{k-1}((4/9)(ρ+6))) give spectral radius >1 and do NOT close. Model B closes but its refill row rests on E_max being a level-independent menu constant — verified at three transitions, not derived from soft counting. So the joint induction does not eliminate the gap; it PINS it to a single scalar (E_max=3) and proves the 4/9 metric contraction converts that one fact into uniform crowding-boundedness.
proofs.level_independence: The constant cannot grow with k for two reasons that combine. (i) STRUCTURAL: the construction is level-HOMOGENEOUS — Stitch_k always draws from the SAME finite connector-word menu (connector_domains4 / dstar5), same max word length (≤4 steps ⇒ ≤3 interior points, each menu step Cheb≤2), attached to anchors that are always M·(triple-free walk). The refill slope E is a property of 'menu-words packed on the spread-out anchor set M·W_k'; the menu is identical at every level, so the constant-SETTING small-ρ packing is literally the same object at every k. (ii) DYNAMICAL: in the closed scalar update C_{k+1}=(4/9)C_k+E, the k-dependence enters ONLY through the (4/9) pull-back of the parent crowding; because 4/9<1 strictly, the map is a contraction with a UNIQUE fixed point 27/5 that every trajectory approaches — the refill source E is added fresh each level but is DISCOUNTED by the geometric factor, so it does not accumulate (Σ(4/9)^j·E converges). This is the whole point of the (4/9) radius-contraction: it turns a per-level bounded refill into a k-independent total. Empirically E_k=3.0 at L6,L7,L8 (dead flat) while C_k rises 2.5→3.0→3.0 and saturates — if refill were the proportional Model-A feedback (E_k=(4/9)a·C_k) E_k would climb with C_k; it does not, confirming the level-independent-source model.
proofs.finite_check: Partial. The binding value E_max=3 is SET at small ρ (max b_k(ρ)/ρ=3.0 attained at ρ=1, and ρ=2 for L8; for ρ≥3 the ratio is ≤2.67 with ≥1 unit of slack) — this small-ρ regime IS a genuine finite menu computation: enumerate the finite menu of connector words and check that (a) the ≤3 interiors of one word can lie within Cheb-1 of a point (giving b(1)=3=max interiors/word) and (b) no two words on anchors at the spacing floor min|M·s|∞=3 stack >6 interiors within Cheb-2. That fixes E_max=3 from the menu alone. What is NOT reduced to a finite computation is the extension b_k(ρ)≤3ρ across the full working range ρ∈[0.5,10] (the recursion consumes ρ up to 10): the mechanism is b_k(ρ) = Σ_{word-arcs meeting the ball}(interiors present) and #{distinct words meeting B(q,ρ)} = #{path arcs} (measured EQUAL at every ρ), so b_k(ρ) ≤ 3·(arc-count) and the envelope needs arc-count ≲ ρ — a 1-D path-crossing bound. That crossing bound is itself a crowding statement (arcs ≤ c(ρ)), not obviously finite. So the finite check genuinely closes the constant-setting regime; the full [0.5,10] envelope is promoted from the 3-level measurement by construction-homogeneity, not by a finite computation. Reduces to: a finite menu check (E_max=3 at ρ≤2) PLUS a residual 1-D incidence claim (arc-count/refill-slope stays ≤3 for ρ∈[3,10], uniformly in k).
proofs.circularity_resolved: Resolved two ways, matching the judge's concern that 'bounding refill in B(q,ρ) pulls in anchors in the larger ball B(q,ρ+4), whose count is the crowding we are proving.' First, those anchors are A^{(k+1)}=M·W_k, so their count is #{W_k ∩ M⁻¹B} = a PARENT (level-k) crowding, already in the induction hypothesis — never the level-(k+1) crowding being proved. The pull-back radius (4/9)(ρ+4) is what appears, strictly smaller than ρ for the working range, so it is consumed by the hypothesis, not the conclusion. Second and more fundamentally, the CLOSING model (B) does not route refill through crowding at all at the coefficient level: the refill slope E is treated as an EXOGENOUS menu constant (the U_B row is [0,0]+source), so the update matrix is strictly lower-triangular-with-zero (spectral radius 4/9 from the crowding row alone). The only coupling is crowding←refill (the '1' in U_B row 1), never refill←crowding, so there is no feedback loop. The det U_B=0 / one-way coupling is the algebraic certificate of non-circularity. (The pessimistic Model A DOES create a feedback refill←crowding via the 16a/81 entry, which is precisely why it fails to close — the refill sub-lemma is exactly the statement that reality follows the acyclic Model B, not the cyclic Model A.)
proofs.computational_evidence: Real construction chains rebuilt from gate2-l7-construction-L{6,7,8}.pkl (27,697 / 92,732 / 311,738 points), anchors tagged vs stitch via the exact disjoint union. Refill envelope b_k(ρ)=max over walk points of #{stitch ∩ Cheb-ρ ball}: b_k(ρ) ≤ 3ρ holds at EVERY ρ and level (tight at ρ=1: b=3=3·1 all levels; tight at ρ=2 L8: b=6=3·2; slack elsewhere). max b_k(ρ)/ρ = 3.000 at L6, L7 AND L8 (dead flat = level-independent E_max). Extended to ρ=20 on L7: b/ρ stays ≤3 (max 3.0 at ρ=1, ~1.9 for ρ≥12) — envelope is set at small ρ and only loosens. OFF-LATTICE sup check (centroid + all 8 half-integer recentrings of the densest stitch balls, ρ=1..4): off-lattice max EQUALS walk-point max, so sup_{q∈R³} is captured. Crowding C_k=max(c_k(ρ)−1)/ρ = 2.5, 3.0, 3.0 (rises then saturates, all ≤ C*=5.4). Joint closure inequality C_{k+1}≤(4/9)C_k+E_k verified: L6→L7 gives 3.0≤4.11, L7→L8 gives 3.0≤4.33. Mechanism: #distinct-words-meeting-ball = #path-arcs at every ρ (measured equal), b≈2.2·words. Effective per-anchor multiplicity a_eff=b(ρ)/#anchors(ρ+4) rises to ~3.5 (>5/4) — confirming Model A does NOT close, isolating the sub-lemma as the real content.
proofs.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/refill/joint_measure.py — builds L6/L7/L8 chains, tags anchors vs stitch, measures c_k(ρ), b_k(ρ), anchor crowding and effective per-anchor multiplicity a_eff over ρ∈[0.5,10]; writes joint_measure.json. Run: /Users/erik/homelab/tools/pypy3.11-v7.3.20-macos_x86_64/bin/pypy3 design/refill/joint_measure.py (~31s). /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/refill/refill_envelope.py — extends ρ to 20, off-lattice sup recentring, and the distinct-words/arcs mechanism on L7 (~38s). /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/refill/joint_update.py — the explicit 2×2 update matrices (Model A vs B), eigenvalues/spectral radius, closing condition, fixed point, and the measured trajectory closure check; writes joint_update.json (plain python3). Artifacts: design/refill/joint_measure.json, design/refill/joint_update.json.
proofs.gap: The joint induction is correctly formalized, non-circular (acyclic Model-B coupling, det U_B=0), and its 4/9 contraction provably converts a level-independent refill slope into uniform crowding c_k(ρ)≤(27/5)ρ+1. The single remaining gap is the refill sub-lemma itself: b_k(ρ)≤3ρ (E_max level-independent) on ρ∈[0.5,10] for ALL k. This is NOT fully delivered by proven ingredients. (i) The provable per-anchor/per-word counting bound (Model A) yields refill slope proportional to crowding with spectral radius (4/9)(1+a)>1 (a≥3 structural), which does NOT close — so soft counting is insufficient; the true bound relies on ANTI-STACKING (distinct words meeting a Cheb-ρ ball ≈ its arc count ≈ ρ, far below the ~(4/3)Cρ anchors in the reach-shell). (ii) The finite check genuinely closes only the small-ρ constant-setting regime (ρ≤2, where E_max=3 = max interiors-per-word is a finite menu computation); the extension to ρ∈[3,10] is promoted from the L6/L7/L8 measurement by construction-homogeneity, not a finite computation. (iii) Root cause: the isotropic ‖M⁻¹‖∞=4/9 Cheb pull-back is too lossy — the exact per-anchor total ratio c/α≈3.4 times 4/9 exceeds 1, so a rigorous closure needs the ANISOTROPIC pull-back ellipsoid (M⁻¹B is thin along non-path directions, effective contraction ~1/3 along the path tangent), i.e. a direction-aware incidence bound. Delivering that anisotropic refill bound (equivalently: arc-count of the path through a Cheb-ρ ball ≤ ρ, uniformly in k) is the exact residual work; it is sharply isolated but remains a genuine all-k claim rather than a finite check.

adversary.surviving_argument: The surviving proof of QUALITATIVE boundedness is an anchor-packing bound that BOTH routes missed by treating the anchor count as the circular quantity c_k. Key lattice fact (verified exhaustively over the v-box +/-8, min attained at v=(-1,-1,-1)): min over ALL nonzero integer v of |M v|_inf = 3, because M=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)) has row0=3v0 and, when v0=0, forces 3|v2| or 3|v1|. Since M is injective (det 27) and walk points are distinct, the anchor set A_k=M.W_{k-1} is 3-SEPARATED for EVERY k, with NO reference to the induction hypothesis. Hence anchor-crowding a_k(q,rho) <= (floor(2rho/3)+1)^3 (each 3x3x3 lattice cell holds <=1 anchor) -- a pure packing number, level-independent and NON-CIRCULAR. For refill: every stitch interior lies Cheb<=4 from its nearer anchor (structural: word <=5 steps of Cheb<=2, nearer anchor <=2 steps away) and each anchor is nearer-anchor to <=g interiors (measured g=3 at L5-L8, structural cap 4 = 2 arcs x <=2 nearer-interiors). So b_k(q,rho) <= g*(floor(2(rho+4)/3)+1)^3. Summing: c_k(q,rho) <= (floor(2rho/3)+1)^3 + g*(floor(2(rho+4)/3)+1)^3, uniform in k, non-circular, PROVEN modulo finite-menu caps. This is a CUBIC ceiling (loose: 257 at rho=1 vs measured 3), but it rigorously proves no blow-up. Measured full-walk c_k confirms it: level-stable and far below (rho=10: 25,26,27,31 across L5-L8; empirical slope ~3rho). What it does NOT give is the TIGHT LINEAR law c_k<=C.rho+1: the isotropic ||M^-1||_inf=4/9 pullback plus g makes the soft-count crowding-recursion coefficient (4/9)(1+g)=20/9 (or 16/9 with per-word f=3) exceed 1, so the linear induction diverges; only the empirically-observed anti-stacking (distinct arcs meeting a Cheb-rho ball ~ rho, not the ~C.rho anchors in the reach-shell) closes it, and that remains unproven.
adversary.spectral_radius: Packing route needs no update-map (direct bound). Route-2 joint 2x2: Model B (menu-source, U_B=[[4/9,1],[0,0]]) spectral radius = 4/9 ~= 0.444 < 1 -> closes to C*=27/5=5.4 (or 7.2 with corrected E_max=4), but its refill row ASSUMES the sub-lemma. Model A (the provable soft-count, U_A=[[4/9,1],[16a/81,4a/9]], det=0) spectral radius = (4/9)(1+a) = 2.0 at measured a~3.5, =1.0 only at marginal a=5/4 -> DIVERGES. So the only self-contained spectral certificate is the packing bound, whose 'radius' is effectively the k-independent packing constant, not a contraction of a circular loop.
adversary.remaining_gap: Two residuals. (1) FINITE CHECK to fully close qualitative boundedness: certify reach-to-nearer-anchor<=4, interiors-per-word<=4, and per-anchor nearer-charge g<=4 over the ENTIRE connector menu (connector_domains4.pkl + dstar5), not just the words realized in L5-L8 -- the structural derivation (word<=5 steps) already implies these, and all three held identically at L5,L6,L7,L8 (g even =3), so this is a mechanical enumeration, not new mathematics. (2) TIGHT LINEAR law (still OPEN, needed only for the sharp availability constant, NOT for no-blow-up): prove b_k(rho) <= C.rho with level-independent C, equivalently that the number of distinct connector-arcs meeting a Cheb-rho ball is O(rho) uniformly in k (anti-stacking). Soft counting gives coefficient (4/9)(1+g)=20/9>1 and diverges; the honest path is the anisotropic M^-1 pullback (M^-1 B is thin off the path tangent, contraction ~1/3 along it) turning the shell overcount into a linear incidence bound. Also a correction to both routes: the literal target C=6 / E_max=3 is FALSE -- the exact sup-over-q gives B_local(1)=4 at L8 (walk-centred sampling undercounted it as 3), so the correct level-independent constant is C>=7.2.
adversary.next: Run the exhaustive connector-menu enumeration (connector_domains4.pkl + dstar5_fragile/band.pkl) to certify reach<=4, interiors<=4, g<=4 over EVERY menu word, closing the finite check and promoting the anchor-packing bound to a full theorem for qualitative crowding-boundedness. Then, for the tight linear law, attack the anti-stacking/arc-incidence bound via the anisotropic M^-1 pullback ellipsoid rather than the isotropic 4/9 Cheb factor.
